ocr.text: In the context of the evolving landscape of the oil and gas industry within the Asia-Pacific region, the management of
decommissioning liabilities has emerged as a critical focal point for operators such as Shell plc. As regulatory frameworks
tighten and stakeholders increasingly emphasize environmental stewardship, the financial implications of decommissioning
activities cannot be overstated. This report aims to provide a comprehensive analysis of decommissioning liability estimates
associated with Shell’s operations across various sites in the region, reflecting projections for the upcoming years 2025, 2026,

and 2027. 

As outlined in the accompanying data table, the estimates for Site A, Site B, and Site C illustrate a systematic
approach to forecasting future financial obligations linked to decommissioning activities. Notably, the 2025 Estimate for Site A
indicates a significant anticipated expenditure, which may be attributed to the aging infrastructure and the complexity of
dismantling operations required in that particular location. Conversely, Site B shows a relatively lower estimate for the same
year, reflecting both the site’s operational efficiency and the strategic planning undertaken to mitigate future costs.

It is essential to assess these estimates not only in isolation but also in the context of broader industry trends, including

advancements in decommissioning technologies and shifts in regulatory expectations.

The data outlined in the table will serve
as an essential tool for stakeholders, providing vital insights into financial planning and risk management strategies associated
with decommissioning activities. Furthermore, understanding these estimates will enable stakeholders to engage in informed
discussions regarding investment and sustainability practices within the sector. As we move closer to the projected timelines,
the accuracy of these estimates will be paramount, requiring ongoing evaluation and adjustments based on real-time operational

data and regulatory changes.

Overall, the implications of these decommissioning liability estimates extend beyond mere
financial forecasts; they encapsulate the critical balance between operational legacy and environmental responsibility. Given the
increasing scrutiny on the oil and gas sector’s environmental impact, proactive engagement with decommissioning strategies
will be essential for Shell and its stakeholders to navigate the complexities of transitioning to a more sustainable energy future.
The ensuing sections of this report will delve deeper into the implications of these estimates, examining their interplay with
Shell's broader corporate sustainability objectives and long-term operational strategies within the Asia-Pacific region.
